assumptions: The issue concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.
The intragroup dividend has been recognised as an intercompany receivable, giving rise to a foreign currency exposure.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including certain foreign currency exposures.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
